The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential applications in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Laurembergia veronicifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
